legislation_pending.title: Act Promoting Investments in Low Carbon Economy
legislation_pending.notes: Consolidated HB 7705 and HB 10633. Requires re-filing in 20th Congress.

implicit_carbon_pricing.mechanism: TRAIN Law fuel excise taxes + coal levy
implicit_carbon_pricing.notes: Implicit carbon price via fuel excise, not explicit carbon pricing

carbon_pricing.notes: Philippines has NO operational carbon tax or ETS on power generation. Effective carbon price is $0/tCO2e. HB 11375 (proposing PHP 1,170/tonne ~$20 initial rate) passed House May 2025 but awaits Senate deliberation and is NOT yet law. TRAIN Law coal excise tax (PHP 150/tonne coal ~$2.60-3.00) is commodity tax, not carbon price, and applies per tonne coal not per tonne CO2 (implied ~$1.30-1.50/tCO2e). DOE carbon credit rules (DC2025-09-0018, October 2025) are voluntary only. For B2 calculation: use $0.
carbon_pricing.source: ICAP; DOF; Congress.gov.ph; PIDS; World Bank Carbon Pricing Dashboard; DOE DC2025-09-0018; verified January 2026

solar_pv_utility.notes: Solar PV is now cheapest source in Philippines

solar_plus_storage.notes: Solar+storage reaches parity with new coal/gas in 2025

offshore_wind.notes: Theoretical estimates - no operational projects yet. First projects target 2028.

gas_ccgt.notes: LNG import dependent after Malampaya depletion

geothermal.notes: Philippines is world's 3rd largest geothermal producer. Competitive with solar.

lcoe_benchmarks.notes: Solar PV ($54/MWh) now cheapest new-build. Solar+storage ($94/MWh) competitive with new coal ($102/MWh) and gas ($96/MWh). Geothermal remains highly competitive ($55/MWh).

malampaya_gas_field.notes: Critical domestic gas source nearing depletion

fuel_costs.notes: 100% coal imported (primarily Indonesia sub-bituminous). Gas from Malampaya (depleting 2027) transitioning to LNG imports.
fuel_costs.source: Indonesian Coal Index; JKM Platts; First Gen investor reports; DOE Philippines

national_targets.net_zero_notes: Philippines is the ONLY Southeast Asian country without an official net-zero target. Aspirational mentions of 'peak by 2030 toward net-zero by 2050' are NOT formal commitments.
national_targets.ndc_conditional_note: Requires international climate finance, technology transfer, capacity building

coal_moratorium.exemptions: Projects with existing DOE endorsements, ECCs, off-grid, PEZA, critical minerals, power crisis
coal_moratorium.october_2025_update: Proponents must submit transition plans consistent with 2060 phase-out

national_targets.notes: No net-zero target (unique in ASEAN). RE target 35% by 2030. Coal moratorium (2020) has major exemptions - 2.6 GW exempted. Coal phase-out target 2060 (very late).
national_targets.source: DOE Philippine Energy Plan 2023-2050; UNFCCC NDC Registry; Climate Action Tracker; DOE Press Releases

wesm_prices.notes: H1 2025 prices at 5-year low due to improved supply and growing RE

transmission.ownership: State Grid China (40%), Monte Oro (30%), Calaca (30%)

mvip.name: Mindanao-Visayas Interconnection Project
mvip.significance: First interconnection of all three Philippine grids

grid_context.reserve_margin_notes: Luzon supply margin fell to 575-641 MW in April 2024 vs NGCP recommended 1,200 MW minimum
grid_context.notes: Fully liberalized wholesale market (WESM). Three grids now interconnected (MVIP 2024). Private sector dominates (unlike EVN, PLN, EGAT). Thin reserve margins.

geothermal.notes: World's 3rd largest geothermal producer. Significant untapped potential.

cbam_exposure.notes: Lack of domestic carbon pricing means Philippine exporters pay full EU CBAM rates

data_quality.notes: Philippines has NO carbon price ($0). Coal moratorium (2020) has major exemptions - coal additions continued post-moratorium. For B2: all utilities PASS at $0 carbon price. For B3: solar+storage ($94/MWh) competitive with coal ($102/MWh) and gas ($96/MWh).

key_investment_considerations: No carbon pricing means B2 automatically passes for all utilities
Coal moratorium (2020) has major exemptions - coal additions continue
Philippines is ONLY ASEAN country without net-zero target
Solar LCOE ($54/MWh) now cheaper than coal ($102/MWh) and gas ($96/MWh)
World's 3rd largest geothermal producer - significant clean energy base
Fully liberalized market (WESM) - private sector dominates unlike peers
Malampaya gas field depleting 2027 - LNG transition underway
Thin reserve margins create supply security concerns
Coal phase-out target 2060 is very late vs global benchmarks
data_sources: Global Energy Monitor Coal Plant Tracker
